input.query: Inconvo semantic model sync CLI or API

content.text:
# Model Commands | Inconvo
created: undefined, last updated: 2026-04-22, [Read more](https://inconvo.com/docs/cli/model/)

Pull, inspect, and mutate your agent's semantic model with the Inconvo CLI.
The `inconvo model` commands let you manage your agent’s semantic model — the configuration that tells Inconvo how to interpret your data.
## model pull
Section titled “model pull”
Pull semantic model files from Inconvo into a local `.inconvo/` directory.
...
|`--agent <agentId>`|Target agent ID (repeatable for multiple agents)|
|`--all-agents`|Pull all agents in the organisation|
|`--connection <connectionId>`|Restrict pull to one connection (requires exactly one `--agent`)|
|`--json`|Print JSON output|
|`--api-key <apiKey>`|API key override|
...
|`--connection <connectionId>`|Connection ID (required for some actions)|
|`--dry-run`|Print resolved payload without applying|
|`--no-sync`|Skip post-mutation sync|
...
The following commands modify specific parts of the semantic model.
They all support `--agent`, `--connection`, `--dry-run`, `--no-sync`, and `--json` options.

# CLI Overview | Inconvo
created: undefined, last updated: 2026-04-22, [Read more](https://inconvo.com/docs/cli/)

Install, authenticate, and use the Inconvo CLI to manage your agents and semantic models.
The Inconvo CLI lets you manage semantic models and database connections directly from your terminal.
It’s designed to work alongside coding agents like Claude Code and Cursor.
...
Or use it via `npx` without installing globally:
...
The CLI uses your Inconvo API key for authentication.
Set it as an environment variable:
Or pass it directly to any command:
## Command Reference
Section titled “Command Reference” |Command|Description|
|--|--|
|`inconvo model pull`|Pull semantic model files into `.inconvo/` for one or more agents|
|`inconvo model agent list`|List agents available in your organisation|
...
|`inconvo connection list`|List connections for an agent|
|`inconvo connection sync`|Trigger a full database resync for a connection|
|`inconvo connection update`|Update a connection description|
...
For detailed documentation on each command group, see:
Model Commands Pull, inspect, and mutate your agent's semantic model.
Connection Commands Manage database connections and trigger resyncs.

# Semantic Model Content...
created: 2026-04-07, last updated: 2026-04-27, [Read more](https://skillsmp.com/skills/inconvoai-inconvo-packages-cli-skill-md)

// Use when updating an Inconvo agent semantic model or user-context from code context.
Covers the full CLI mutation workflow — tables, columns, relations, conditions, policies, virtual tables, computed columns, and user-context — using `inconvo model` and `inconvo connection` commands.
...
- **Post-mutation sync**: automatic refresh of local `.inconvo/` files after a successful mutation.
- **`model pull`**: rebuild local snapshots from the platform.
- **`connection sync`**: ask the platform to re-introspect a connection's database schema.
Follow with `model pull` when you need updated local files.
...
|What you want to do|CLI Command|
|--|--|
|Update connection description|`connection update --agent <agentId> --connection <connectionId> --description "..."`|
|Pull latest snapshot|`model pull --agent <agentId> [--connection <connectionId>]`|
|Trigger DB resync|`connection sync --agent <agentId> --connection <connectionId>`|
...
It does not perform a remote DB rescan; `connection sync` does that.
...
# 4.

# Launch HN: Inconvo (YC S23) – AI agents for customer-facing analytics
created: 2025-08-22, last updated: 2025-08-23, [Read more](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=44984096)

Inconvo is a platform built from the ground up for developers building AI agents for customer-facing analytics.
We make it simple to expose data to Inconvo by connecting to SQL databases.
We offer a semantic model to create a layer that governs data access and defines business logic, conversation logs to track user interactions, and a developer-friendly API for easy integration.
...
Our API is designed for multi-tenant databases, allowing you to pass session information as context.
This instructs the agent to only analyse data relevant to the specific tenant making the request.
...
Yes we just create the chart data, the front end is responsible for rendering and can choose the library.
We will respond with a consistent chart object (https://inconvo.com/docs/api-reference/conversations/respons...) that can then be transformed with your own code to fit the spec of the frontend chart library.
...
The reason we don't is that we currently use Drizzle for schema introspection and query building and Drizzle doesn't have an adapter for ClickHouse yet.
There's an active issue on the Drizzle repo requesting Clickhouse support that has some interest and the possibility of using the Postgres interface that ClickHouse exposes was discussed there.

# Inconvo - Natural Language SQL Agent SDK | EveryDev.ai
created: 2026-03-03, last updated: 2026-04-11, [Read more](https://www.everydev.ai/tools/inconvo)

Inconvo is an open-source platform for building customer-facing data agents that let users query production SQL databases through natural language.
...
Inconvo integrates via API/SDK or as an MCP server, and supports databases including PostgreSQL, MySQL, Microsoft SQL Server, Amazon Redshift, BigQuery, and Supabase.
- **Semantic Layer** — *Define how agents interpret your data, enforce permissions, apply business rules, and scope access per tenant without writing custom middleware.*
...
- **API & SDK Integration** — *Integrate agents into your existing codebase using the Node.js SDK or REST API with response streaming support.*
...
- **CLI Quickstart** — *Run `npx inconvo@latest dev` to start locally and get your first data agent running in under five minutes.*
- **Assistant-UI & Vercel AI SDK** — *Pre-built integrations with popular frontend frameworks for embedding chat interfaces directly into your product.*
...
- REST API

# inconvo CDN by jsDelivr - A CDN for npm and GitHub
created: 2026-04-07, last updated: 2026-04-22, [Read more](https://www.jsdelivr.com/package/npm/inconvo)

inconvo
inconvoai

CLI for Inconvo development environment

# Inconvo Launches: Ask-AI for Customer-Facing Analytics - Fondo
created: 2025-03-14, last updated: 2025-12-11, [Read more](https://www.fondo.com/blog/inconvo-launches)

With Inconvo, shipping AI-first analytics is easy.
Just send a user question to the API, and it will respond with a chart, table, or text.
...
**Inconvo** ** provides a single API call to power genuinely intelligent Ask AI for customer-facing analytics.**
Behind this API call, Inconvo handles all the hard problems of Ask AI for customer-facing analytics.
Inconvo connects to your database to automatically retrieve the right data to answer your users’ questions.
Then, you’re in charge of presenting the API responses to your users in a way that fits your design.
**Inconvo is purpose-built to overcome the challenges of Ask-AI for customer-facing analytics**, providing tools that handle multi-tenancy, database access control, AI evaluation, and a semantic layer.

# Synchronize a Power BI semantic model scale-out replicas
created: 2025-07-22, last updated: 2025-09-03, [Read more](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/enterprise/powerbi/service-premium-scale-out-sync-replica)

To check the sync status of your replicas, use the `SyncStatus` REST API.
This article describes the PowerShell commands for using this API.
...
{
...
}
...
3. Get the semantic model Id by running the command below.
Replace `<WorkspaceId>` with the Id of your workspace.
…
```
...
```
...
5. Sync the read-write semantic model and read-only replicas by using the following command.
Replace the values of `<WorkspaceId>` and `<DatasetId>` accordingly.
```
Invoke-PowerBIRestMethod -Url 'groups/<WorkspaceId>/datasets/<DatasetId>/queryScaleOut/sync' -Method Post -Body "" | ConvertFrom-Json | Format-List # Replace <WorkspaceId> with the Id of your workspace and <DatasetId> with the Id of your semantic model
```
...
6. To verify the sync is complete, run the `syncStatus` command in *step 4* again.
You might need to run the command a few times depending on the length of the time that's required to sync the semantic model copies.
When the sync is complete, check the values of `syncStartTime` and `syncEndTime ` to see how long the sync took.

input.query: Inconvo CLI model mutation commands example

content.text:
# Model Commands | Inconvo
created: undefined, last updated: 2026-04-22, [Read more](https://inconvo.com/docs/cli/model/)

Pull, inspect, and mutate your agent's semantic model with the Inconvo CLI.
...
|`--dry-run`|Print resolved payload without applying|
|`--no-sync`|Skip post-mutation sync|
...
## Mutation Commands
Section titled “Mutation Commands”
The following commands modify specific parts of the semantic model.
They all support `--agent`, `--connection`, `--dry-run`, `--no-sync`, and `--json` options.
|Command|Description|
|--|--|
|`inconvo model table`|Set table access mode (queryable, joinable, off) and table prompts|
|`inconvo model column`|Toggle columns on/off|
|`inconvo model computed`|Create, update, or delete computed columns|
|`inconvo model relation`|Toggle relations on/off|
|`inconvo model condition`|Manage conditions on tables|
|`inconvo model policy`|Manage access policies|
|`inconvo model virtual`|Manage virtual tables|
|`inconvo model user-context`|Configure user context fields and context filters|

# Semantic Model Content...
created: 2026-04-07, last updated: 2026-04-27, [Read more](https://skillsmp.com/skills/inconvoai-inconvo-packages-cli-skill-md)

|Rename / add notes / hide a column|`model column update --rename --notes --selected`|
|Set a column unit|`model column set-unit --unit USD`|
|Create a computed column|`model computed create --name --ast <json> --unit`|
|Update a computed column|`model computed update` / `model computed set-unit`|
|Delete a computed column|`model computed delete`|
|Toggle an FK relation on/off|`model relation toggle --selected true|false`|
|Create a manual relation|`model relation manual create --source-table --target-table --name --is-list --pair`|
|Delete a manual relation|`model relation manual delete --source-table --relation`|
...
--json
# Hide sensitive column
npx inconvo@latest model column update \
--agent <agentId> --connection <connectionId> \
--table <table> --column password --selected false --json
# Set currency unit
npx inconvo@latest model column set-unit \
--agent <agentId> --connection <connectionId> \
--table <table> --column price --unit USD --json
```

# CLI Overview | Inconvo
created: undefined, last updated: 2026-04-22, [Read more](https://inconvo.com/docs/cli/)

## Command Reference
Section titled “Command Reference” |Command|Description|
|--|--|
|`inconvo model pull`|Pull semantic model files into `.inconvo/` for one or more agents|
...
For detailed documentation on each command group, see:
Model Commands Pull, inspect, and mutate your agent's semantic model.

# Inconvo: The Revolutionary Data Agent Platform Every Developer ...
created: 2026-03-18, last updated: 2026-05-02, [Read more](https://converter.brightcoding.dev/blog/inconvo-the-revolutionary-data-agent-platform-every-developer-needs)

```
import "dotenv/config";
import Inconvo from "@inconvoai/node";
...
const inconvo = new Inconvo({
apiKey: process.env.INCONVO_API_KEY, // Load from environment variables
});
...
```
# This single command starts the entire Inconvo platform locally
npx inconvo@latest dev
# Expected output:
# ✔ Inconvo Engine started on port 26686
# ✔ Dashboard available at http://localhost:26686
# ✔ API documentation at http://localhost:26686/docs
# ✔ Press Ctrl+C to stop
```
**What happens**: The CLI downloads the Inconvo engine, starts the query validation service, permission manager, and observability dashboard.
It auto-discovers local databases and suggests schema configurations.
The `@latest` tag ensures you always run the newest stable version.
...
**Permission Composition** enables complex security models.
Combine row-level filters (`tenant_id = ${userContext.tenantId}`) with column-level restrictions (`hide: [users.ssn, users.salary]`).
Use **permission profiles** to create reusable sets of rules for different user roles: `admin_profile`, `manager_profile`, `customer_profile`.

# inconvo CDN by jsDelivr - A CDN for npm and GitHub

""" Send bogus JSON with an instance"""
self.name = new_name
return await self.save()
async def update(self):
return await self.client.put(self.url, json=self.data)
cli = APIClient.cli

class YourObject(YourClient.Model):

Base class for mutable value fields like
Basically, this field:
caches the externalized value, so that you can mutate it
marks the field as dirty so you get the internalized mutated value that next time you get the
cache_get(

# GitHub - dojo-modeling/dojo-cli: A command-line interface to for black box model execution.
created: 2021-12-16, last updated: 2025-06-01, [Read more](https://github.com/dojo-modeling/dojo-cli)

The library has 7 key methods:
- List the latest versions of all available models.
- Print parameter metadata for a selected model.
- Print a summary of the output and accessory files of a selected model.
- Print a desription of a selected model.
- Get the results of a detached model run that has finished.
- Run a model.
- List all versions of a model.
...
### Parameters
...
--model
...
: name of the model
...
--config
...
### Example
$
```
dojo listmodels
```
...
Prints a description of model parameters and writes an example to file.
...
### Example
```

Add gross_margin measure to inconvo.yaml
Create index.js to query the Data Agent

input.result:
I have successfully defined the `gross_margin` calculated measure and queried the Data Agent.

### Changes made:
1.  **Updated `inconvo.yaml`**: Added the `gross_margin` measure to the `orders` table with the formula `total_amount - cost`.
2.  **Created `index.js`**: Implemented a Node.js script that:
    *   Loads environment variables from `.env`.
    *   Initializes the Inconvo client using `@inconvoai/node`.
    *   Creates a new conversation with the necessary `userIdentifier` and `userContext` (required by the agent).
    *   Queries the agent with the message: `"What is the average gross margin per month?"`.
    *   Saves the JSON response to `response.json`.
3.  **Executed the script**: Ran `node index.js` which successfully communicated with the Inconvo Cloud API and generated the `response.json` file.

### Verification:
The `response.json` file contains a structured response from the Inconvo agent, including the average gross margin per month.

```json
{
  "id": "47c07171-e419-4451-b980-1077a2457a39",
  "conversationId": "convo_d5d3a68e-f1fd-4d1c-823a-79680b809e6e",
  "type": "table",
  "message": "Average gross margin per month (based on sales).",
  "table": {
    "head": [
      "month",
      "avg_gross_margin"
    ],
    "body": [
      [
        "2026-01",
        "30"
      ],
      [
        "2026-02",
        "45"
      ]
    ]
  }
}
```

The task is complete.
